title: Alaska Native Tribal Health Consortium leader working toward hospital ownership
summary: Yereth Rosen Alaska Beacon With a new leader at the helm, the Alaska Native Tribal Health Consortium is working toward a long-held goal: ownership of the Anchorage hospital that it already manages. The Alaska Native Medical Center, the state’s largest Native hospital, stands out as the only facility in the tribal consortium’s sprawling Midtown campus that is owned by the Indian Health Service. That split between ownership and management has turned out to be burdensome, said Natasha Singh, the Alaska Native Tribal Health Consortium’s newly appointed president and c...

title: Are Arctic Viruses “Zombies”?
summary: Melting ice can free long-frozen ‘zombie viruses.' The nickname may perpetuate misunderstandings about the real threat of Arctic disease. The post Are Arctic Viruses “Zombies”? appeared first on The Arctic Institute - Center for Circumpolar Security Studies .
